plan: 1. Add bloom parameters to QualityProfile in src/visual/quality.ts — Add `enableBloom: boolean`, `bloomStrength: number`, `bloomThreshold: number`, `bloomRadius: number` to QualityProfile interface. Set low tier: enableBloom=false. Medium tier: enableBloom=true, strength ~0.8, threshold ~0.6, radius ~0.4. High tier: enableBloom=true, strength ~1.2, threshold ~0.4, radius ~0.6.

2. Create src/visual/composer.ts — New module that initializes EffectComposer with RenderPass + UnrealBloomPass from three/examples/jsm/postprocessing. Export an `initComposer(renderer, scene, camera, qualityProfile)` function that returns `{ composer, bloomPass }` or null if bloom is disabled. Also export a `resizeComposer(composer, width, height)` helper.

3. Integrate composer into main.ts — After initScene() and quality resolution, call initComposer() with the resolved quality profile. Pass the composer (or null) into startLoop() and attachResizeHandler().

4. Update src/visual/renderLoop.ts — Accept optional EffectComposer in startLoop dependencies. Replace the single `renderer.render(scene, camera)` call at line 264 with: if composer exists, `composer.render()`, else `renderer.render(scene, camera)`. This preserves the fallback path for low tier.
5. Update src/visual/resize.ts — Extend attachResizeHandler to accept optional EffectComposer. On resize, call `composer.setSize(width, height)` alongside the existing renderer size update.

6. Verify all modes render correctly — Run dev server, confirm all existing geometry modes still render through the composer pipeline without visual regression or WebGL errors. Test with bloom enabled (medium/high) and disabled (low).
7. Build and validate — Run `npm run build` to confirm clean production build. Check browser console for zero shader compile errors, zero WebGL validation errors, and stable first-frame rendering.
testing_recommendations: Verify no WebGL errors or shader compile errors in browser console on load with each quality tier (low/medium/high)
Confirm bloom is visually disabled on low tier — scene should render identically to pre-bloom baseline
Confirm bloom is visually active on medium/high tiers — bright points should glow softly
Resize the browser window and confirm no rendering artifacts or size mismatches (composer must track viewport)
Test all existing geometry modes still render correctly through the EffectComposer pipeline
Test with audio playing — verify bloom interacts well with audio-reactive brightness peaks
Run `npm run build` and confirm zero build errors
Load the production build via `npm run preview` and verify stable first frame with no console errors
risks: UnrealBloomPass adds GPU overhead — must be fully disabled (not just strength=0) on low tier to avoid unnecessary render passes
EffectComposer requires the renderer to have a specific pixel ratio and size at construction time — must ensure initialization order is correct (quality resolved before composer created)
Bloom threshold tuning is subjective — initial values may need iteration to match the luminous reference image quality
Three.js r183 postprocessing imports use ES module paths (three/examples/jsm/postprocessing/) — verify Vite resolves these correctly without additional config
